prompt: Wide panoramic scene split into four vignettes connected by flowing dotted lines. Far left: a parent on a couch scrolling past an irrelevant ad on their phone, mildly annoyed expression. Center-left: a bakery owner in an apron standing behind a counter, looking confused and frustrated at a laptop showing a complicated ad dashboard with too many charts. Center-right: a journalist at a desk in a small newsroom, writing on a laptop with a worried, pensive expression, resting chin on hand, a monitor behind her shows a declining revenue graph. Far right: a young woman entrepreneur in Nairobi (Kenyan, dark skin, natural hair in a puff, wearing a bright blouse) at a simple desk in a sunlit workspace with shipping boxes behind her, looking determined but overwhelmed at a laptop — she wants to reach customers but the tools are too complex. All four people should look concerned, frustrated, or pensive — NOT happy or smiling. The mood is empathetic but somber. The four scenes flow into each other naturally via dotted lines. No text, words, or labels in the image.
alt_text: Four everyday people affected by advertising: a consumer seeing irrelevant ads, a bakery owner struggling with complexity, a journalist worried about declining ad revenue, and an entrepreneur in Nairobi overwhelmed by the tools
